name: sqlmap-database-penetration-testing
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "automate SQL injection testing," "enumerate database structure," "extract database credentials using sqlmap," "dump tables and columns from a vulnerable database," or "perform automated database penetration testing." It provides comprehensive guidance for using SQLMap to detect and exploit SQL injection vulnerabilities.
capabilities: Guide automated SQL injection vulnerability detection using SQLMap
Demonstrate database enumeration workflows for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and MSSQL
Explain table and column discovery techniques
Document data extraction methods for authorized security testing
Provide advanced exploitation techniques for privilege escalation
Teach WAF bypass methods using tamper scripts
author: zebbern
securitySummary: All 130 detected patterns are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill is legitimate security documentation for SQLMap, an industry-standard penetration testing tool. All 'external_commands' findings are bash code examples in markdown blocks demonstrating authorized SQLMap usage. All 'network' findings are example URLs for testing environments. The skill explicitly requires written authorization and includes legal disclaimers for authorized testing only.
valueStatement: Automate SQL injection detection and database exploitation testing. Learn systematic penetration testing workflows for authorized security assessments.
userTitle: Master SQLMap for SQL Injection Testing
limitations: Requires pre-installed SQLMap tool on the system
Only works on databases with confirmed SQL injection vulnerabilities
Cannot bypass all Web Application Firewalls or intrusion prevention systems
Time-based blind SQL injection attacks are significantly slower than other techniques
Must have explicit written authorization before testing any production systems

useCases.title: Penetration Testing Workflow
useCases.description: Security professionals use this skill to systematically test web applications for SQL injection vulnerabilities during authorized penetration testing engagements.

useCases.title: Vulnerability Assessment
useCases.description: Security analysts integrate these SQLMap techniques into automated vulnerability scanning workflows to identify database security flaws in their organization's applications.

useCases.title: Security Education
useCases.description: Students and researchers use this documentation to understand SQL injection attack vectors and learn how to defend against them in secure development practices.

promptTemplates.title: Basic SQL Injection Test
promptTemplates.prompt: Test the target URL http://testphp.vulnweb.com/artists.php?artist=1 for SQL injection vulnerabilities and enumerate all available databases.
promptTemplates.scenario: Initial vulnerability assessment on an authorized testing environment

promptTemplates.title: Extract User Credentials
promptTemplates.prompt: Using the confirmed SQL injection point at http://target.com/page.php?id=1, extract the username and password columns from the users table in the webapp database.
promptTemplates.scenario: Data extraction during authorized penetration testing

promptTemplates.title: POST Request Injection
promptTemplates.prompt: I have a Burp Suite captured request saved in login.txt. Analyze this POST request for SQL injection vulnerabilities in the username parameter and enumerate the database structure.
promptTemplates.scenario: Testing login forms for SQL injection flaws

promptTemplates.title: Advanced OS Shell Access
promptTemplates.prompt: The target at http://192.168.1.10/admin.php?id=1 has a SQL injection vulnerability with DBA privileges. Demonstrate how to gain OS shell access and read the /etc/passwd file.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced privilege escalation in isolated lab environment

bestPractices: Always obtain explicit written authorization before performing any SQL injection testing on production systems or third-party applications.
Use the --batch flag for automated testing environments to prevent interactive prompts from blocking long-running scans.
Start with low --level and --risk settings (1-2) before escalating to aggressive tests (level=5, risk=3) to avoid triggering WAFs prematurely.
antiPatterns: Never run SQLMap against systems without clear authorization. Unauthorized testing is illegal and unethical.
Avoid using --google-dork or -g flags to scan random websites. Always limit testing to approved targets within engagement scope.
Do not use --dump-all on large production databases without approval. This can cause significant performance impact and service disruption.

faq.question: Is SQLMap legal to use?
faq.answer: SQLMap is legal when used for authorized penetration testing on systems you own or have explicit written permission to test. Using SQLMap against unauthorized targets is illegal.

faq.question: What permissions do I need for SQL injection testing?
faq.answer: You must have written authorization from the system owner or a contract clearly defining the scope of testing. Educational testing should only use legal practice environments.

faq.question: Can SQLMap bypass all Web Application Firewalls?
faq.answer: No. SQLMap includes tamper scripts to bypass some WAFs, but modern security systems can detect and block SQLMap traffic. Testing against protected systems requires advanced techniques.

faq.question: What is the --batch flag used for?
faq.answer: The --batch flag makes SQLMap run non-interactively by using default answers for all prompts. This is essential for automated testing and CI/CD integration.

faq.question: How long does a SQLMap scan take?
faq.answer: Scan time varies from seconds for basic detection to hours or days for full database extraction. Time-based blind injection is significantly slower than error-based or boolean-based techniques.

faq.question: Can SQLMap damage my database?
faq.answer: SQLMap is primarily designed for data extraction, but commands like --os-shell and file operations can modify system state. Always test in non-production environments first and use --technique flags to limit risk.
